   355→    }
   356→
   357→    // Create directories
   358→    ensureDir(config.outputDir);
   359→    ensureDir(config.intermediateDir);
   360→
   361→    // Start the pipeline
   362→    const pipelineStartTime = Date.now();
   363→    logger.info("Starting subtitle translation pipeline");
   364→    if (config.processOnlyPart !== undefined) {
   365→      logger.info(
   366→        chalk.magentaBright(
   367→          `--- Processing ONLY Part ${config.processOnlyPart} ---`
   368→        )
   369→      );
   370→    }
   371→    logger.debug(
   372→      `Configuration: ${JSON.stringify(
   373→        { ...config, apiKeys: { gemini: "***", anthropic: "***" } },
   374→        null,
   375→        2
   376→      )}`
   377→    );
   378→    let currentChunks: ChunkInfo[] = [];
   379→    const allIssues: ProcessingIssue[] = [];
   380→    let videoDuration: number | null = null; // Store video duration
   381→
   382→    // --- Cost Tracking Initialization ---
   383→    const costBreakdown: CostBreakdown = {
   384→      totalCost: 0,
   385→      transcriptionCost: 0,
   386→      translationCost: 0,
   387→      costPerModel: {},
   388→      warnings: [],
   389→    };
   390→
   391→    // --- Step 1: Split ---
   392→    logger.info(chalk.blueBright("--- Step 1: Splitting Inputs ---"));
   393→    videoDuration = await getVideoDuration(config.videoPath);
   394→    if (!videoDuration) {
   395→      logger.warn(
   396→        "Could not determine video duration. Cost per minute will be unavailable."
   397→      );
   398→    }
   399→    const splitResult = await split({
   400→      videoPath: config.videoPath,
   401→      srtPath: config.srtPath,
   402→      outputDir: config.intermediateDir,
   403→      chunkDuration: config.chunkDuration,
   404→      chunkOverlap: config.chunkOverlap,
   405→      chunkFormat: config.chunkFormat,
   406→      maxConcurrent: config.maxConcurrent,
   407→      force: config.force,
   408→      processOnlyPart: config.processOnlyPart,
   409→      inputOffsetSeconds: config.inputOffsetSeconds,
   410→    });
   411→    currentChunks = splitResult.chunks;
   412→    allIssues.push(...splitResult.issues);
   413→    if (currentChunks.filter((c) => c.status !== "failed").length === 0) {
   414→      logger.error("Splitting failed for all chunks. Aborting pipeline.");
   415→      // TODO: Add final report generation here
   416→      process.exit(1);
   417→    }
   418→
   419→    // Filter chunks early if processOnlyPart is set
   420→    let relevantChunks = currentChunks;
   421→    if (config.processOnlyPart !== undefined) {
   422→      relevantChunks = currentChunks.filter(
   423→        (c) => c.partNumber === config.processOnlyPart
   424→      );
   425→      if (relevantChunks.length === 0) {
   426→        logger.error(
   427→          `Specified part ${config.processOnlyPart} not found after splitting. Aborting.`
   428→        );
   429→        process.exit(1);
   430→      }
   431→      logger.info(
   432→        `Focusing on ${relevantChunks.length} chunk(s) for part ${config.processOnlyPart}.`
   433→      );
   434→    }
   435→
   436→    // --- Step 2: Transcribe & Adjust ---
   437→    logger.info(
   438→      chalk.blueBright("--- Step 2: Transcription & Timestamp Adjustment ---")
   439→    );
   440→    const transcribeResult = await transcribe(relevantChunks, config);
   441→    allIssues.push(...transcribeResult.issues);
   442→    // --- Merge Step 2 Results ---
   443→    currentChunks = currentChunks.map((originalChunk) => {
   444→      const updated = transcribeResult.chunks.find(
   445→        (tc) => tc.partNumber === originalChunk.partNumber
   446→      );
   447→      return updated || originalChunk; // Return updated chunk if found, else original
   448→    });
   449→    // Update relevantChunks based on the *merged* currentChunks
   450→    relevantChunks =
   451→      config.processOnlyPart !== undefined
   452→        ? currentChunks.filter((c) => c.partNumber === config.processOnlyPart)
   453→        : currentChunks;
   454→    // --- End Merge ---
   455→    if (relevantChunks.filter((c) => c.status === "prompting").length === 0) {
   456→      logger.error(
   457→        "Transcription/Adjustment failed for all targeted chunks. Aborting pipeline."
   458→      );
   459→      process.exit(1);
   460→    }
   461→
   462→    // --- Calculate Transcription Costs ---
   463→    logger.debug("Calculating transcription costs...");
   464→    let transcriptionTokensFound = false;
   465→    currentChunks.forEach((chunk) => {
   466→      // Check for comprehensive cost tracking first
   467→      if (chunk.totalTranscriptionCost !== undefined) {
   468→        transcriptionTokensFound = true;
   469→        const modelName = config.transcriptionModel;
   470→        costBreakdown.transcriptionCost += chunk.totalTranscriptionCost;
   471→        costBreakdown.totalCost += chunk.totalTranscriptionCost;
   472→        costBreakdown.costPerModel[modelName] =
   473→          (costBreakdown.costPerModel[modelName] || 0) +
   474→          chunk.totalTranscriptionCost;
   475→      }
   476→      // Fall back to single attempt cost if comprehensive tracking isn't available
   477→      else if (
   478→        chunk.llmTranscriptionInputTokens !== undefined &&
   479→        chunk.llmTranscriptionOutputTokens !== undefined
   480→      ) {
   481→        transcriptionTokensFound = true;
   482→        const modelName = config.transcriptionModel;
   483→        const cost = calculateCost(
   484→          modelName,
   485→          chunk.llmTranscriptionInputTokens,
   486→          chunk.llmTranscriptionOutputTokens
   487→        );
   488→        costBreakdown.transcriptionCost += cost;
   489→        costBreakdown.totalCost += cost;
   490→        costBreakdown.costPerModel[modelName] =
   491→          (costBreakdown.costPerModel[modelName] || 0) + cost;
   492→      } else {
   493→        if (
   494→          relevantChunks.some(
   495→            (rc) =>
   496→              rc.partNumber === chunk.partNumber &&
   497→              rc.status !== "splitting" &&
   498→              rc.status !== "pending"
   499→          )
   500→        ) {
   501→          const warnMsg = `Token count unavailable for transcription model: ${config.transcriptionModel}`;
   502→          if (!costBreakdown.warnings.includes(warnMsg)) {
   503→            costBreakdown.warnings.push(warnMsg);
   504→          }
   505→        }
   506→      }
   507→    });
   508→    if (!transcriptionTokensFound && relevantChunks.length > 0) {
   509→      logger.warn(
   510→        "Could not find transcription token counts for any processed chunks."
   511→      );
   512→    }
   513→    // --- End Cost Calculation ---
   514→    relevantChunks =
   515→      config.processOnlyPart !== undefined
   516→        ? currentChunks.filter((c) => c.partNumber === config.processOnlyPart)
   517→        : currentChunks;
   518→    if (relevantChunks.filter((c) => c.status === "prompting").length === 0) {
   519→      logger.error(
   520→        "Transcription/Adjustment failed for all targeted chunks. Aborting pipeline."
   521→      );
   522→      process.exit(1);
   523→    }
   524→
   525→    // --- Step 3: Translate ---
   526→    logger.info(chalk.blueBright("--- Step 3: Generating Translations ---"));
   527→    const translateResult = await translate(relevantChunks, config);
   528→    allIssues.push(...translateResult.issues);
   529→    // --- Merge Step 3 Results ---
   530→    currentChunks = currentChunks.map((originalChunk) => {
   531→      const updated = translateResult.chunks.find(
   532→        (tc) => tc.partNumber === originalChunk.partNumber
   533→      );
   534→      return updated || originalChunk; // Return updated chunk if found, else original
   535→    });
   536→    // Update relevantChunks based on the *merged* currentChunks
   537→    relevantChunks =
   538→      config.processOnlyPart !== undefined
   539→        ? currentChunks.filter((c) => c.partNumber === config.processOnlyPart)
   540→        : currentChunks;
   541→    // --- End Merge ---
   542→    // Check status on the potentially filtered relevantChunks
   543→    if (relevantChunks.filter((c) => c.status === "completed").length === 0) {
   544→      logger.error(
   545→        "Translation/Validation failed for all targeted chunks. Aborting pipeline."
   546→      );
   547→      process.exit(1);
   548→    }
   549→
   550→    // --- Calculate Translation Costs ---
   551→    logger.debug("Calculating translation costs...");
   552→    let translationTokensFound = false;
   553→    currentChunks.forEach((chunk) => {
   554→      // Check for comprehensive cost tracking first
   555→      if (chunk.totalTranslationCost !== undefined) {
   556→        translationTokensFound = true;
   557→        const modelName = config.translationModel;
   558→        costBreakdown.translationCost += chunk.totalTranslationCost;
   559→        costBreakdown.totalCost += chunk.totalTranslationCost;
   560→        costBreakdown.costPerModel[modelName] =
   561→          (costBreakdown.costPerModel[modelName] || 0) +
   562→          chunk.totalTranslationCost;
   563→      }
   564→      // Fall back to single attempt cost if comprehensive tracking isn't available
   565→      else if (
   566→        chunk.llmTranslationInputTokens !== undefined &&
   567→        chunk.llmTranslationOutputTokens !== undefined
   568→      ) {
   569→        translationTokensFound = true;
   570→        const modelName = config.translationModel;
   571→        const cost = calculateCost(
   572→          modelName,
   573→          chunk.llmTranslationInputTokens,
   574→          chunk.llmTranslationOutputTokens
   575→        );
   576→        costBreakdown.translationCost += cost;
   577→        costBreakdown.totalCost += cost;
   578→        costBreakdown.costPerModel[modelName] =
   579→          (costBreakdown.costPerModel[modelName] || 0) + cost;
   580→      } else {
   581→        if (
   582→          relevantChunks.some(
   583→            (rc) =>
   584→              rc.partNumber === chunk.partNumber &&
   585→              rc.status !== "prompting" &&
   586→              rc.status !== "pending"
   587→          )
   588→        ) {
   589→          const warnMsg = `Token count unavailable for translation model: ${config.translationModel}`;
   590→          if (!costBreakdown.warnings.includes(warnMsg)) {
   591→            costBreakdown.warnings.push(warnMsg);
   592→          }
   593→        }
   594→      }
   595→    });
   596→    if (!translationTokensFound && relevantChunks.length > 0) {
   597→      logger.warn(
   598→        "Could not find translation token counts for any processed chunks."
   599→      );
   600→    }
   601→    // --- End Cost Calculation ---
   602→    relevantChunks =
   603→      config.processOnlyPart !== undefined
   604→        ? currentChunks.filter((c) => c.partNumber === config.processOnlyPart)
   605→        : currentChunks;
   606→    if (relevantChunks.filter((c) => c.status === "completed").length === 0) {
   607→      logger.error(
   608→        "Translation/Validation failed for all targeted chunks. Aborting pipeline."
   609→      );
   610→      process.exit(1);
   611→    }
   612→
   613→    // --- Step 4: Finalize Subtitles ---
   614→    logger.info(chalk.blueBright("--- Step 4: Finalizing Subtitles ---"));
   615→    const finalizeResult = await finalize(currentChunks, config);
   616→    allIssues.push(...finalizeResult.issues);
   617→    const finalSrtPath = finalizeResult.finalSrtPath;
   618→
   619→    // --- Pipeline Complete ---
   620→    // Calculate final cost metrics
   621→    if (videoDuration && videoDuration > 0) {
   622→      costBreakdown.costPerMinute =
   623→        (costBreakdown.totalCost / videoDuration) * 60;
   624→    }
   625→
   626→    // --- Final Report Output ---
   627→    if (finalSrtPath) {
   628→      logger.success(chalk.greenBright("Pipeline completed successfully!"));
   629→      let reportContent = `Pipeline Summary:
   630→`;
   631→      reportContent += `- Final SRT: ${finalSrtPath}
   632→`;
   633→      reportContent += `- Intermediate Files: ${config.intermediateDir}
   634→`;
   635→      reportContent += `- Total Issues Logged: ${allIssues.length}
   636→`;
   637→      // Cost Report
   638→      reportContent += `\n--- Estimated Cost Breakdown ---
   639→`;
   640→      reportContent += `- Total: $${costBreakdown.totalCost.toFixed(4)}
   641→`;
   642→      reportContent += `- Transcription: $${costBreakdown.transcriptionCost.toFixed(
   643→        4
   644→      )} (Model: ${config.transcriptionModel})
   645→`;
   646→      reportContent += `- Translation: $${costBreakdown.translationCost.toFixed(
   647→        4
   648→      )} (Model: ${config.translationModel})
   649→`;
   650→      if (costBreakdown.costPerMinute !== undefined) {
   651→        reportContent += `- Cost Per Minute of Video: $${costBreakdown.costPerMinute.toFixed(
   652→          4
   653→        )}
   654→`;
   655→      }
   656→      // Per-model breakdown if multiple distinct models were used
   657→      if (Object.keys(costBreakdown.costPerModel).length > 1) {
   658→        reportContent += `- Cost Per Model Details:
   659→`;
   660→        for (const [model, cost] of Object.entries(
   661→          costBreakdown.costPerModel
   662→        )) {
   663→          reportContent += `    - ${model}: $${cost.toFixed(4)}
   664→`;
   665→        }
   666→      }
   667→      if (costBreakdown.warnings.length > 0) {
   668→        reportContent += `\n- Cost Warnings:\n`;
   669→        costBreakdown.warnings.forEach(
   670→          (w: string) => (reportContent += `    - ${w}\n`)
   671→        );
   672→      }
   673→      reportContent += `(Note: Costs are estimates based on token counts reported by APIs where available.)`;
   674→
   675→      console.log(
   676→        boxen(reportContent, {
   677→          padding: 1,
   678→          margin: 1,
   679→          borderColor: "green",
   680→          title: "Pipeline Summary",
   681→        })
   682→      );
   683→    } else {
   684→      logger.error(
   685→        chalk.redBright(
   686→          "Pipeline completed, but failed to generate final SRT file."
   687→        )
   688→      );
   689→      console.log(
   690→        boxen(
   691→          `Final SRT generation failed.\nCheck logs and intermediate files: ${config.intermediateDir}\nTotal Issues Logged: ${allIssues.length}`,
   692→          {
   693→            padding: 1,
   694→            margin: 1,
   695→            borderColor: "red",
   696→            title: "Pipeline Failed",
   697→          }
   698→        )
   699→      );
   700→      process.exit(1);
   701→    }
   702→  } catch (err: any) {
   703→    logger.error(`Fatal pipeline error: ${err.message || err}`, err.stack);
   704→    console.error(
   705→      boxen(
   706→        chalk.red(
   707→          `Fatal Pipeline Error: ${err.message || err}\n${err.stack || ""}`
   708→        ),
   709→        { padding: 1, margin: 1, borderColor: "red" }
   710→      )
   711→    );
   712→    process.exit(1);
   713→  }
   714→}
   715→
   716→// Always run main when this file is executed
   717→main();
   718→
   719→export { main };

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary of the chunking logic. Let me compile the findings:

## Complete Chunking Logic Analysis for IPGU Pipeline

I've traced the entire data flow from video → chunks → transcripts → translations. Here's the complete picture:

### 1. **Splitter: How It Works** (`src/splitter/index.ts` + `src/splitter/video_splitter.ts`)

**Chunk Calculation (`src/utils/time_utils.ts` - `calculateChunks()`)**:
- Takes: `totalDurationSeconds`, `chunkDurationSeconds`, `overlapSeconds`
- Algorithm:
  - Starts at `startTime = 0`
  - Each chunk spans `[startTime, min(startTime + chunkDuration, totalDuration)]`
  - **Overlap mechanism**: Next chunk's `startTime = endTime - overlapSeconds`
  - Special handling: If the final chunk is < 1/3 of chunk duration, it merges with the previous chunk
- Returns `TimeChunk[]` with `startTimeSeconds`, `endTimeSeconds`, `partNumber`

**Media Extraction (`splitVideo()`)**:
- Creates media chunks using ffmpeg with start/stop times
- Supports two formats: MP3 (audio only, 192kbps) or MP4 (video 640x360, 128kbps audio)
- Chunks are named `part{number}.{format}` (zero-padded: `part01.mp3`, `part02.mp3`, etc.)
- Optional SRT splitting for subtitle alignment

**Key Detail**: **Chunks DO overlap** - if you have a 1200s (20min) video with 120s overlap, chunk N covers seconds [N×(1200-120), N×(1200-120)+1200]

---

### 2. **Transcriber: How It Consumes Chunks** (`src/transcriber/index.ts`)

**Processing Model**:
- **Processes one chunk at a time** — each chunk is transcribed independently
- **NO context from neighboring chunks** — the transcriber only sees the current chunk's media file
- Loads media from `chunk.mediaChunkPath` and transcribes it using Gemini API
- No cross-chunk context or overlap handling in transcription

**Output Structure**:
1. Raw transcript saved to: `raw_llm_transcripts/part{N}_raw.txt`
2. Adjusted transcript (timestamps shifted) saved to: `transcripts/part{N}_adjusted.txt`
   - Timestamps are adjusted by `chunk.startTimeSeconds` to align with original video timing
   - Formula: `timestamp_adjusted = raw_timestamp + chunk.startTimeSeconds`

**Concurrency**: Controlled by `config.maxConcurrent` (default 5)

---

### 3. **Translator: How It Consumes Chunks** (`src/translator/index.ts`)

**Processing Model**:
- **Processes one chunk at a time** — each chunk's transcript is translated independently
- **NO context from neighboring chunks** in the default flow
- Reads adjusted transcript from `chunk.adjustedTranscriptPath` 
- Generates translation prompt using the chunk's transcript content
- Calls LLM (Gemini or Claude) with the prompt

**Data Flow per Chunk**:
1. **Prompt Generation**: Creates prompt from adjusted transcript + config
2. **LLM Call**: 
   - API retry loop (configurable: `config.retries` attempts with exponential backoff)
   - Validation retry loop (configurable: `config.retries` attempts)
   - Special fallback for last chunk: if all validation attempts fail on the final chunk, uses the longest successfully parsed response
3. **Parsing**: Extracts structured translation data (no context sharing)
4. **Validation**: Checks completeness, timing alignment, etc.
5. **Output**: Parsed translations saved to `parsed_data/part{N}_parsed.json`

**Concurrency**: Controlled by `config.maxConcurrent` (default 5)

**No Context Sharing**: Each chunk's translation is completely independent — previous/next chunks don't influence the translation.

---

### 4. **Presets in `src/main.ts`**

Two preset configurations:

**Preset "2.5" (Gemini 2.5 for all)**:
```
- transcriptionModel: "gemini-2.5-pro-preview-03-25"
- translationModel: "gemini-2.5-pro-preview-03-25"
- maxConcurrent: 12
- chunkDuration: 1200s (20 minutes)
- chunkOverlap: 120s (2 minutes)
- chunkFormat: "mp3"
- retries: 3 (both API and validation)
- transcriptionRetries: 3
```

**Preset "2.5-claude" (Gemini + Claude)**:
```
- transcriptionModel: "gemini-2.5-pro-preview-03-25"
- translationModel: "claude-3-7-sonnet-latest"
- maxConcurrent: 3
- chunkDuration: 600s (10 minutes)
- chunkOverlap: 60s (1 minute)
- chunkFormat: "mp3"
- retries: 3 (both API and validation)
- transcriptionRetries: 3
```

CLI defaults (if no preset):
- `chunkDuration: 600s`
- `chunkOverlap: 120s`
- `maxConcurrent: 5`

---

### 5. **Chunk Context & Overlap Mechanism**

**Overlap Use**:
- **Splitter**: Creates overlapping time ranges in video extraction (e.g., chunk 1 covers 0-1200s, chunk 2 covers 1080-2280s with 120s overlap)
- This means the audio/video in the overlap region appears in both chunks

**Transcriber**: 
- Processes overlapping audio independently 
- Timestamps are adjusted back to original video time
- Overlapping transcripts will naturally occur for the same audio segment, but they're produced separately

**Translator**:
- Receives adjusted transcripts (with global timestamps)
- Each chunk translated independently — no merging or deduplication of overlapping content
- The pipeline assumes finalization step will handle overlap reconciliation

**Summary**: Chunks overlap at the **media extraction level** to provide context continuity in audio (in case LLMs need to understand cross-boundary speech patterns), but **the transcription and translation processors don't explicitly leverage this overlap** — they process chunks independently and rely on downstream finalization to merge/deduplicate.

---

### Data Flow Diagram

```
Video File
    ↓
[SPLITTER] calculateChunks()
  ├─ Creates overlapping time ranges
  ├─ Extracts overlapping media chunks (ffmpeg)
  └─ Saves: part01.mp3, part02.mp3, ... (with overlap)
    ↓
[TRANSCRIBER] transcribe()
  ├─ Reads: part01.mp3, part02.mp3, ...
  ├─ NO context between chunks
  ├─ Transcribes each independently
  └─ Saves: part01_adjusted.txt, part02_adjusted.txt
    ↓
[TRANSLATOR] translate()
  ├─ Reads: part01_adjusted.txt, part02_adjusted.txt, ...
  ├─ NO context between chunks
  ├─ Translates each independently
  └─ Saves: part01_parsed.json, part02_parsed.json
    ↓
[FINALIZER] (not fully shown)
  └─ Merges chunks into final SRT
```

---

### Key Takeaways

1. **Overlap exists at media level** but is NOT actively used by transcriber/translator
2. **No cross-chunk context sharing** — each chunk is processed in isolation
3. **Timestamps are globally adjusted** in transcriber to map back to original video time
4. **Concurrency is chunk-level** — multiple chunks process in parallel
5. **Retry logic** is applied per-chunk and includes validation retries (not just API retries)
6. **Last chunk special handling**: Translator uses fallback logic to prefer longest valid response if all validation attempts fail on the final chunk


CLI defaults (if no preset):
- `chunkDuration: 600s`
- `chunkOverlap: 120s`
- `maxConcurrent: 5`

---

### 5. **Chunk Context & Overlap Mechanism**

**Overlap Use**:
- **Splitter**: Creates overlapping time ranges in video extraction (e.g., chunk 1 covers 0-1200s, chunk 2 covers 1080-2280s with 120s overlap)
- This means the audio/video in the overlap region appears in both chunks

**Transcriber**: 
- Processes overlapping audio independently 
- Timestamps are adjusted back to original video time
- Overlapping transcripts will naturally occur for the same audio segment, but they're produced separately

**Translator**:
- Receives adjusted transcripts (with global timestamps)
- Each chunk translated independently — no merging or deduplication of overlapping content
- The pipeline assumes finalization step will handle overlap reconciliation

**Summary**: Chunks overlap at the **media extraction level** to provide context continuity in audio (in case LLMs need to understand cross-boundary speech patterns), but **the transcription and translation processors don't explicitly leverage this overlap** — they process chunks independently and rely on downstream finalization to merge/deduplicate.

---

### Data Flow Diagram

```
Video File
    ↓
[SPLITTER] calculateChunks()
  ├─ Creates overlapping time ranges
  ├─ Extracts overlapping media chunks (ffmpeg)
  └─ Saves: part01.mp3, part02.mp3, ... (with overlap)
    ↓
[TRANSCRIBER] transcribe()
  ├─ Reads: part01.mp3, part02.mp3, ...
  ├─ NO context between chunks
  ├─ Transcribes each independently
  └─ Saves: part01_adjusted.txt, part02_adjusted.txt
    ↓
[TRANSLATOR] translate()
  ├─ Reads: part01_adjusted.txt, part02_adjusted.txt, ...
  ├─ NO context between chunks
  ├─ Translates each independently
  └─ Saves: part01_parsed.json, part02_parsed.json
    ↓
[FINALIZER] (not fully shown)
  └─ Merges chunks into final SRT
```

---

### Key Takeaways

1. **Overlap exists at media level** but is NOT actively used by transcriber/translator
2. **No cross-chunk context sharing** — each chunk is processed in isolation
3. **Timestamps are globally adjusted** in transcriber to map back to original video time
4. **Concurrency is chunk-level** — multiple chunks process in parallel
5. **Retry logic** is applied per-chunk and includes validation retries (not just API retries)
6. **Last chunk special handling**: Translator uses fallback logic to prefer longest valid response if all validation attempts fail on the final chunk

Here's the original IPGU chunking logic:

**Splitting:** Video gets split into overlapping chunks via ffmpeg. Default chunk sizes depend on preset:
- Preset `2.5` (Gemini): **20 min** chunks, 2 min overlap
- Preset `2.5-claude`: **10 min** chunks, 1 min overlap
- Small final chunks (<1/3 of chunk duration) get merged into the previous chunk

**Transcription:** Each chunk transcribed **independently** — no cross-chunk context. Timestamps get shifted back to absolute video time afterward.

**Translation:** Each chunk translated **independently** — no cross-chunk context. One LLM call per chunk.

**Overlap:** The audio overlap exists (same audio appears in adjacent chunks), but neither the transcriber nor translator does anything with it. It's just insurance for audio that straddles a boundary. The finalizer presumably deduplicates later.

So the original IPGU code already chunks at 10-20 min, not 5 min. Our experiment artificially used smaller chunks than IPGU's defaults.

message.content: But wait ... did our results not show better preference for the 10% chunk relative to the other ...? Please tell me i am not misinterpreting this, be honest

content.text:
You're not misinterpreting it. The Run 2 results showed 10% winning 48/100. But the judges flagged that most of those "wins" were:

1. **Ties defaulting to A** — all 4 levels produced identical translations (same source transcript, same model), judge picks A by convention
2. **All-wrong matches** — none of the 4 levels matched the reference line (timing misalignment), judge defaults to A again

So 10% didn't win because small chunks translate *better*. It won because the evaluation method is broken — it defaults to A when it can't distinguish. The actual signal (cases where translations genuinely differed) was buried in noise.

The result is essentially **null** — no measurable difference between levels. Which makes sense: all levels used the exact same transcripts from the same 5-min audio chunks. The only thing that varied was how much *surrounding text* the translator saw, and apparently that didn't matter much for this content.

But this also means we can't claim larger context *hurts*. It's inconclusive, not negative.

input.command:
cd /root/southbridge/ipgu/data/output/context_sweep_nosrt/rankings && python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json, re

for chunk_num in range(1, 11):
    chunk_id = f"chunk{chunk_num:02d}"
    
    # Read verdict
    with open(f"{chunk_id}_verdict.json") as f:
        verdicts = json.load(f)
    
    # Read samples
    with open(f"{chunk_id}_samples.txt") as f:
        sample_text = f.read()
    
    # Parse sample lines
    lines = []
    for block in re.split(r'--- Line \d+', sample_text):
        m_ts = re.search(r'\(([^)]+)\)', block)
        m_ref = re.search(r'REFERENCE: "(.*?)"', block)
        m_a = re.search(r'A \(10%\):\s+"(.*?)"', block)
        m_b = re.search(r'B \(25%\):\s+"(.*?)"', block)
        m_c = re.search(r'C \(33%\):\s+"(.*?)"', block)
        m_d = re.search(r'D \(50%\):\s+"(.*?)"', block)
        if m_ref:
            lines.append({
                'ts': m_ts.group(1) if m_ts else '?',
                'ref': m_ref.group(1),
                'A': m_a.group(1) if m_a else '',
                'B': m_b.group(1) if m_b else '',
                'C': m_c.group(1) if m_c else '',
                'D': m_d.group(1) if m_d else '',
            })
    
    # Only show lines where 10% won
    for v in verdicts:
        if v['winner'] != 'A':
            continue
        idx = v['line'] - 1
        if idx >= len(lines):
            continue
        L = lines[idx]
        
        print(f"{'='*90}")
        print(f"CHUNK {chunk_num} LINE {v['line']} ({L['ts']})  —  Judge reason: {v['reason']}")
        print(f"{'='*90}")
        print(f"  REF:   {L['ref']}")
        print(f"  ✓ 10%: {L['A']}")
        print(f"    25%: {L['B']}")
        print(f"    33%: {L['C']}")
        print(f"    50%: {L['D']}")
        print()

PYEOF

message.content: Okay I think some of these still look super off. Is japanese translatio usually this bad on gemini or genuiine linguistic ambiguities?

content.text:
Honestly, a lot of these aren't translation quality issues — they're **alignment issues**. The reference SRT and the LLM's transcript are slicing the dialogue at different points, so the judge is comparing lines that correspond to different moments in the conversation.

Look at chunk 3 line 4:
```
REF:   Ah, yes.
10%:   Oh, right. I'm about to take your father his dinner...
```

That's not a bad translation — the LLM merged "Ah, yes" with the next few sentences into one subtitle. The translation of the actual Japanese is fine, it's just a different segmentation.

Same with chunk 5 lines 9-10: both reference lines (`All right` and `I really appreciate your help`) matched to the same translation (`He should be here by then, too`) — completely different dialogue, just close timestamps.

The genuinely interesting cases are where the same source line produces different English:

- **Chunk 4 line 10:** 10% says "Nodokatei wouldn't have to close" (correct), 25/33/50% say "you don't have to worry about that" (different source line entirely — the larger context window matched to a *different* nearby line)
- **Chunk 9 line 9:** 10% gets "A photo of me and Kou-kun!" while 25/33/50% just say "Huh?" — larger context windows seem to have dropped this line

So to answer your question: this isn't Gemini being bad at Japanese. The actual translations are reasonable. The problem is **subtitle segmentation** — where the LLM decides to split/merge lines — which makes time-based comparison unreliable.

Want me to do a quick spot check by pulling some raw Japanese transcript lines and comparing them against the translations to confirm?

message.content: No the actual translations are horrible. But almost certainly not a context length problem. Actually can you rerun the test with the correct fallback logic in case of those errors -> which is to throw out that test case?

content.text:
I can do this without re-running judges — just filter the existing data. Throw out cases where:
1. All 4 translations are identical (tie)
2. Any translation is "(no match)"
3. All translations are clearly misaligned (judge said "none match" / "all equally wrong")

thrown = []

for chunk_num in range(1, 11):
    chunk_id = f"chunk{chunk_num:02d}"
    
    with open(f"{chunk_id}_verdict.json") as f:
        verdicts = json.load(f)
    
    with open(f"{chunk_id}_samples.txt") as f:
        sample_text = f.read()
    
    # Parse sample lines
    lines = []
    for block in re.split(r'--- Line \d+', sample_text):
        m_ref = re.search(r'REFERENCE: "(.*?)"', block)
        m_a = re.search(r'A \(10%\):\s+"(.*?)"', block)
        m_b = re.search(r'B \(25%\):\s+"(.*?)"', block)
        m_c = re.search(r'C \(33%\):\s+"(.*?)"', block)
        m_d = re.search(r'D \(50%\):\s+"(.*?)"', block)
        m_ts = re.search(r'\(([^)]+)\)', block)
        if m_ref:
            lines.append({
                'ts': m_ts.group(1) if m_ts else '?',
                'ref': m_ref.group(1),
                'A': m_a.group(1) if m_a else '',
                'B': m_b.group(1) if m_b else '',
                'C': m_c.group(1) if m_c else '',
                'D': m_d.group(1) if m_d else '',
            })
    
    for v in verdicts:
        idx = v['line'] - 1
        if idx >= len(lines):
            continue
        L = lines[idx]
        reason = v.get('reason', '')
        
        # Throw out conditions
        throw = False
        throw_reason = None
        
        # 1. All identical
        if L['A'] == L['B'] == L['C'] == L['D']:
            throw = True
            throw_reason = "all identical"
        
        # 2. Any "(no match)"
        elif "(no match)" in [L['A'], L['B'], L['C'], L['D']]:
            throw = True
            throw_reason = "has (no match)"
        
        # 3. Judge flagged as all wrong / misaligned
        elif any(phrase in reason.lower() for phrase in [
            "none match", "all equally wrong", "all are a different line",
            "all are mismatched", "all equally mismatched",
            "forced pick", "no valid winner",
            "all say essentially the same",
        ]):
            throw = True
            throw_reason = f"judge: {reason[:60]}"
        
        if throw:
            thrown.append({
                'chunk': chunk_num, 'line': v['line'],
                'winner': v['winner'], 'throw_reason': throw_reason,
                'ref': L['ref'][:50]
            })
        else:
            kept.append({
                'chunk': chunk_num, 'line': v['line'],
                'winner': v['winner'], 'reason': reason,
                'ref': L['ref'][:50],
                'A': L['A'][:40], 'B': L['B'][:40],
                'C': L['C'][:40], 'D': L['D'][:40],
            })

# Summary
print(f"KEPT: {len(kept)} / {len(kept)+len(thrown)}  |  THROWN OUT: {len(thrown)}")
print()

# Tally kept
level_map = {'A': '10%', 'B': '25%', 'C': '33%', 'D': '50%'}
tally = {'10%': 0, '25%': 0, '33%': 0, '50%': 0}
for k in kept:
    level = level_map.get(k['winner'], k['winner'])
    tally[level] = tally.get(level, 0) + 1

print("FILTERED TALLY (valid comparisons only)")
print("=" * 50)
total = len(kept)
for level, count in sorted(tally.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
    bar = "█" * round(count / max(total, 1) * 40)
    print(f"  {level:5s}  {count:3d} / {total}  ({count/total*100:.0f}%)  {bar}")

# Per-chunk
print()
print("PER-CHUNK (filtered)")
print(f"{'Chunk':>8}  {'10%':>4}  {'25%':>4}  {'33%':>4}  {'50%':>4}  {'kept':>5}")
print("-" * 42)
for c in range(1, 11):
    ck = [k for k in kept if k['chunk'] == c]
    ct = {l: sum(1 for k in ck if level_map.get(k['winner']) == l) for l in ['10%', '25%', '33%', '50%']}
    print(f"{'chunk'+str(c).zfill(2):>8}  {ct['10%']:4d}  {ct['25%']:4d}  {ct['33%']:4d}  {ct['50%']:4d}  {len(ck):5d}")

print()
print(f"THROWN OUT BREAKDOWN ({len(thrown)} cases):")
reasons = {}
for t in thrown:
    r = t['throw_reason'].split(':')[0].strip()
    reasons[r] = reasons.get(r, 0) + 1
for r, c in sorted(reasons.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
    print(f"  {r}: {c}")

print()
print("KEPT CASES (for review):")
print("=" * 100)
for k in kept:
    print(f"  C{k['chunk']:02d} L{k['line']:02d}  winner={level_map.get(k['winner'],'?'):4s}  REF: {k['ref']}")
    print(f"         10%: {k['A']}")
    print(f"         25%: {k['B']}")
    print(f"         33%: {k['C']}")
    print(f"         50%: {k['D']}")
    print(f"         reason: {k['reason'][:80]}")
    print()

PYEOF

content.text:
After throwing out 45 invalid cases (36 misaligned, 9 identical):

**FILTERED TALLY (55 valid comparisons)**

| Level | Wins | % |
|---|---|---|
| 10% | 19 | 35% |
| 33% | 14 | 25% |
| 25% | 13 | 24% |
| 50% | 9 | 16% |

10% still leads, but the gap narrowed significantly (48% → 35%). And looking at the kept cases, many of the remaining 10% "wins" are hairsplitting — like `Morning` vs `- Morning` (chunk 6 line 1) or matching a name spelling.

The real signal: **no clear winner across context levels**. 10/25/33% are within noise of each other. 50% is slightly worse, possibly because longer prompts cause the model to be less precise. But with only 55 valid samples, none of this is statistically significant.

message.content:
Sigh. Lets just fork and commit. I give up. I thought there was a quick win here but Ive already spent too long on this

Can you find the chat convo for this from ~/.claude

COpy it here in this folder

Then spin off 4 opus subagents to look over each part of the chat and put it into A_STORY_OF_WOE.md. Dramatize our misadventure and mis-assumptions, our fall as dramatic as that of Wagner's heroes who reached out to touch ... anyways, please make a summary of our convo with our mistaken assumptions and the rabbit it lead us down, but be sure to document that our hypothesis was at least correct in direction, had the data *observed* been correct
